notes: BIB: volume 151 correct; missing doi/url. CLAIM ISSUE: Paper states Amoukou et al. 'provide tighter bounds on the approximation error of tree SHAP values when the independence assumption fails.' The actual paper focuses on computing *exact* (not bounded) Shapley values for tree models more accurately using conditional expectations — it is not primarily about bounding approximation error. The claim conflates accuracy-of-computation with bounding-of-error. Overstated but directionally related.
